description: A web-based parametric calculator that brings embodied-carbon feedback into early-stage structural design.
body: CarbonFrame lets designers quickly define the key parameters of a structural framework — floor count, bay dimensions, slab thickness — and assign concrete, steel or timber sections to each element. The tool returns a clear breakdown of embodied carbon emissions, material mass and quantity takeoff, with a side-by-side comparison mode for weighing design iterations against each other.

Models export as .3dm files carrying both the geometric and the analytical model, ready for FEM analysis. Version 2.0 aims at additional building typologies — such as industrial structures — and built-in FEM analysis.
